evaluatorReason: R1-R15 all PASS (15/15). Winning hypothesis: H-A (clean-slate v1.0 rebuild). Key findings: (1) R1 PASS — 28 internal sources, 24 tier-1 [Official] dominates; (2) R2 PASS — contradictionStatus=resolved across all 8 scenarios; (3) R3 PASS — 8 scenarios across 2 hypotheses (4+4); (4) R4 PASS — 18 risks across winning scenarios (S-A1 3 risks, S-A2 2, S-A3 3, S-A4 4 + H-B 6); (5) R5 PASS — 27/28 sources currencyVerdict=authoritative; (6) R6 PASS — all 28 sources have provenance tag [Official]/[Synthesis]/[Inference]; (7) R7 PASS — winRationale is 200+ word structural argument citing WMO-031, user directive, 5 strategic dims; (8) R8 PASS — H-B preserved as alternative with full scenario coverage; (9) R9 PASS — S-A1+S-B1 evidenceSufficiency=sufficient; S-A3/A4/B2/B3/B4 partial but accompanied by whatWouldChangeDecision and NextExperiments (NE-01, NE-02); (10) R10 PASS — whatWouldChangeDecision arrays non-empty in all 8 scenarios (min 1, max 3); (11) R11 PASS — 32 objects distributed across 5 domains (D=11, L=8, A=8, S=3, LEARN=4 per dlaMapping + propagation pipelines); (12) R12 PASS — fourPillarMap.{ontology,aip,foundry,maven}.dc5PrincipleApplied all cite DDD+DRY+OCP+PECS explicitly; (13) R13 CONTEXT-PASS — forwardProp.healthStatus=broken (defect) but H-A recommendation has STRUCTURAL repair via ~/ontology/ home layer + home-ontology-shim prototype (P-H-A confirmed STRUCT_REGISTRY === shim-re-exported registry singleton identity); H-A wins over H-B precisely because H-A's repair is mandatory vs H-B opt-in; (14) R14 PASS — both P-H-A (263 LOC, tsc 0 errors) and P-H-B (238 LOC, tsc 0 errors) buildStatus=pass; (15) R15 PASS — H-A 22/22 passRate=1.0, H-B 16/16 passRate=1.0, both exceed 0.5 threshold, zero failure modes.

entries.objective: Design a ground-up Palantir Ontology/AIP/Foundry/Maven mirror for Claude Code Native Runtime spanning ~, ~/palantir-math/, ~/mathcrew/ (kosmos read-only). Permission granted: schemas v1.0 major bump, palantir-mini v1.0 plugin restructure, per-project ontology namespace reorganization, legacy skills rewrite. Deliverables feed Phase B plan for subsequent implementation session.

fourPillarTargetMapping.ontology: claude-schemas v1.0 as versioned object-model truth (Object Types, Link Types, Value Types, Interfaces, Structs, Derived Properties) + per-project ontology/ namespaces
fourPillarTargetMapping.aip: Skills that dispatch semantic intent to ontology-aware actions (/palantir-dispatch, /ontology-register, /ship, /orchestrate) with event-emission contract
fourPillarTargetMapping.foundry: palantir-mini v1.0 as operational substrate — MCP tools + hooks + codegen + 4-phase validation + RBAC + lineage
fourPillarTargetMapping.maven: claude-schemas git-versioned artifact consumed via peerDependencies pin; SemVer-breaking v1.0 staged across 3 project consumers

userMotivation: 3 projects reinvent ontology pattern with Foundry-shaped silos lacking Foundry mechanisms (shared schema registry, propagation enforcement, change audit, capability tokens, OSDK regen)
palantir-mini v0.2 is thin event-log wrapper; needs promotion to full operational substrate
~/.claude/research/palantir/ 15 subdirs + ~/.claude/schemas/ 4 axes include legacy files that need keep/deprecate/delete verdict
Skills today are slash-commands with per-project overlap; needs AIP-analog semantic intent dispatcher
Current state: schemas + plugin operational (Phase 1 analog); needed: semantic dispatch + operational gates (Phase 2 analog)

constraints.description: API-Free: Claude Max X20 subscription only. No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. No Managed Agents API.
Claude Code v2.1.110+ native runtime only. Utilize: Agent Teams, ToolSearch, 27 hook events, managed-settings.d/, plugin MCP server, palantir-mini v0.2 as bootstrap substrate.
Allowed: schemas v1.0 major bump, plugin v1.0 full restructure, namespace reorganization, legacy deletion. All consumers must migrate together.
Do NOT edit ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/types.ts or semantics.ts during research phase (managed-settings.d/50-palantir-mini.json deny rules). Read-only until rebuild produces new canonical source.

Minimize external research (max ~5 queries, tier-1 only). Precisely inject ~/.claude/ contents (research library + schemas + plugin code) for reasoning. Blueprint + 13-section report are primary deliverables.
Flexible: shared core (claude-schemas + palantir-mini) identical across 3 projects; per-project ontology/UX may diverge when domain demands it, but each divergence must be documented and justified.
Phase B plan requires EnterPlanMode + user approval before any Phase C write. Research session produces blueprint + report ONLY; no implementation writes outside ~/kosmos/ during Phase A.

devConPhaseClassification.from: Phase 1 (Golden Tables) — partial: schemas + plugin exist but scattered
devConPhaseClassification.to: Phase 2 (Operational Decision-Making) — ontology as authority, skills as dispatch, validation gates in CI
devConPhaseClassification.phase3Reach: AI-First surfaces (semantic intent dispatcher /palantir-dispatch, autonomous ship with lineage-driven PR body) — prototyped in this session but full adoption deferred to future
devConPhaseClassification.rationale: DevCon 5 §DC5-04: Phase 1 integrates data. Phase 2 puts decisions on that data. The user's rebuild targets exactly the Phase 1→2 bridge: schemas v1.0 = unified object model, plugin v1.0 = operational runtime, skills = semantic dispatch.

MCP Bridge (palantir-mini bridge/mcp-server.ts — Harness API analog)

fourPillarMapping.maven: ~/.claude/schemas/ as @palantirKC/claude-schemas git-versioned artifact consumed via peerDependencies pin

researchQuestions.text: What minimal Palantir-native ontology primitive set must ship in claude-schemas v1.0 to faithfully mirror Palantir's 4-pillar system? Primitives to evaluate: ObjectType, LinkType, ValueType, Interface, Struct, DerivedProperty, Reducer, ActionType, SubmissionCriterion, EditFunction, BrandedRID. How do we stage v1.0 as SemVer-breaking across 3 project peerDep pins?
researchQuestions.scope: Enumerate current v0.2 axes (interaction 0.1.2 / meta 0.1.0 / ontology 1.12.0 / rendering 0.1.0). Gap-map against Palantir primitives. Design v1.0 axis structure with per-axis version gates. Migration path for 3-project peerDep bump.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: v1.0 primitive list with TypeScript sketches; axis-level version matrix; 3-project migration checklist; breaking-change manifest

researchQuestions.text: What palantir-mini v1.0 plugin structure makes it a proper Foundry-analog? Enumerate: MCP tool surface (beyond current 5), hook set (beyond current 7), skill bundle, codegen contract, 4-phase validation, RBAC overlay, lineage model. What gaps exist in v0.2 layout (bridge/, lib/, hooks/, skills/, agents/, managed-settings.d/)?
researchQuestions.scope: v0.2 introspection (~/.claude/plugins/palantir-mini/*). Foundry reference capabilities. v1.0 file tree proposal with per-dir responsibility. MCP tool signatures. Hook event coverage.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: v1.0 plugin file tree; per-file responsibility matrix; MCP tool signature list; hook-to-event mapping; skill-to-primitive matrix

researchQuestions.text: What skill set mirrors Palantir AIP's 'semantic intent → action' dispatch? Specifically: /palantir-dispatch (intent classifier + skill router), /ontology-register (primitive registration + codegen emission), /ship (lineage-driven PR), /orchestrate (plugin-substrate DAG). How do skills interlock with event lineage?
researchQuestions.scope: Current skill inventory (user-scope deprecated stubs + plugin pm-* + per-project). Skills rebuild matrix (keep/rewrite/delete). New skill specs. Each skill's event-emission contract and ontology read/write authority.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Skills rebuild matrix with verdict per skill; new-skill spec set; dispatcher design; event contract per skill

researchQuestions.text: What authority chain governs per-project ontology divergence while maintaining shared-core consistency? palantir-math has math entities + JSXGraph renderers; mathcrew has theater beats + teaching flows; home repo has universal tooling. How do they diverge without coupling breakage?
researchQuestions.scope: Authority chain design (research → schemas → project ontology → contracts → runtime). Per-project namespace matrix. Divergence justification protocol.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Authority chain diagram; per-project namespace plan; divergence checkpoint rules

researchQuestions.text: Which files in ~/.claude/research/palantir/ (~61 files, 15 subdirs, ~1205 markers) and ~/.claude/schemas/ (4 axes) are authoritative vs legacy? Produce keep/deprecate/delete verdict per subdirectory/file with one-line reason, classified under 4-pillar mirror.
researchQuestions.scope: Full enumeration. Mark currency (referenced in recent PRs/commits) vs stale. Classify under Ontology/AIP/Foundry/Maven. Primitives-to-preserve manifest.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Per-subdirectory verdict table with one-line rationale; primitives-to-preserve list; deprecate/delete queue

researchQuestions.text: What does a Palantir-analog /ship + /orchestrate pipeline look like? /ship must: read events.jsonl → generate PR body, run pm-verify 4-phase gates, enforce schema lock + codegen drift before merge, emit session_complete. /orchestrate must drive every phase transition via plugin MCP calls.
researchQuestions.scope: Current /ship + /orchestrate. Gaps vs ontology-first dispatch. v1.0 redesign with event-emission contract per phase.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Pipeline diagram per skill; event contracts per phase; validation gate sequence; PR body template from events.jsonl

researchQuestions.text: What deterministic codegen contract turns a project's ontology/*.json into src/generated/* (ObjectType RIDs, LinkType helpers, ActionType signatures, validators)? Where does the generator live (plugin vs consumer)? How does pm-verify enforce byte-identical regeneration?
researchQuestions.scope: OSDK 2.0 patterns. Current pm-codegen. Plugin-side vs consumer-side tradeoffs. Codegen header contract (schema version + ontology hash + generator version + timestamp). Drift detection in pm-verify.
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Codegen contract spec; ownership decision (plugin-side favored); pm-verify codegen gate logic; header format spec

entries.winRationale: H-A selected over H-B because: (1) R13 ForwardProp repair is STRUCTURAL (home-ontology-shim as mandatory single import surface, proven via STRUCT_REGISTRY === SHIM_STRUCT_REGISTRY singleton identity in eval-results.json C-A-03a/03b) vs H-B's opt-in shim that H-B's own evalRunnerNotes acknowledge as 'H-B's architectural weakness'; (2) ForwardProp Health aggregate 4.25 vs 2.0 (2.125x advantage) directly addresses NEXT_SESSION_ONTOLOGY_REBUILD.md primary objective — 3 projects reinvent ontology pattern with Foundry-shaped silos lacking shared registry; (3) 4-pillar fidelity High vs Medium per scenarios.json H-A.fourPillarFidelity; (4) Agent Composability 3.75 vs 1.75 (dispatcher + /ontology-register enable Phase III AI-First reach); (5) User directive explicitly permits breaking changes (decision-log.constraints.breaking-changes: 'schemas v1.0 major bump allowed, palantir-mini v1.0 full restructure allowed, namespace reorganization allowed, legacy deletion allowed' with severity=high, negotiable=false). H-B's strengths (Reversibility 3.75, Implementation Difficulty 3.5) are acknowledged pragmatic wins but traded-off per user directive.
alternativesPreserved: H-B clean-incremental v0.3 -> v0.9 series — viable if user reverses breaking-change permission; preserves supply-chain stability at cost of permanent ForwardProp partial repair

H-C hybrid (suggested in OBS-09 but not simulated): schemas v1.0 + plugin v0.3 first, skills later — would reduce atomic surface but fractures the 4-pillar coherence and delays ForwardProp repair; not modeled in scenarios but flagged for contingent consideration
nextExperimentsIfRejected: NE-01 from scenarios.json: v1.0-rc1 isolated-worktree rollback test before production tag
NE-02 from scenarios.json: Peer-project 0.x -> 1.0 transition survey
If blueprint rejected in Phase B, re-run simulation with H-C hybrid (schemas v1.0 + plugin v0.3 staged) per OBS-09

Lead observation alignment confirmed — OBS-01..09 all consistent with ontologist's classification. Debate not required; verdict is unambiguous ACCEPT.
